title: Print finishing and binding workers
description: Set up, maintain, and dismantle audio and video equipment, such as microphones, sound speakers, connecting wires and cables, sound and mixing boards, video cameras, video monitors and servers, and related electronic equipment for live or recorded events, such as concerts, meetings, conventions, presentations, podcasts, news conferences, and sporting events. Set up, operate, and maintain the electronic equipment used to acquire, edit, and transmit audio and video for radio or television programs. Control and adjust incoming and outgoing broadcast signals to regulate sound volume, signal strength, and signal clarity. Operate satellite, microwave, or other transmitter equipment to broadcast radio or television programs. Assemble and operate equipment to record, synchronize, mix, edit, or reproduce sound, including music, voices, or sound effects, for theater, video, film, television, podcasts, sporting events, and other productions. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Set up or operate bindery machines, such as coil binders, thermal or tape binders, plastic comb binders, or specialty binders.
Cut binder boards and cover material to specified dimensions, using board shears, hand cutters, or cutting machines.
Form book bodies by folding and sewing printed sheets to create signatures and assemble them in numerical order.
Imprint or emboss lettering, designs, or numbers on book covers using gold, silver, or colored foil and stamping machines.
Insert book bodies in devices that form back edges into convex shapes and produce grooves for cover attachment.
Maintain records, such as daily production and quantities of processing completed, using specified forms.
Monitor machine operations to detect malfunctions, adjustments, and ensure proper working condition.
Set up or operate machines that perform binding operations, such as pressing, folding, or trimming, and troubleshoot issues as needed.
Stitch or glue endpapers, bindings, backings, or signatures using sewing machines or glue machines.
Trim edges of books to size using cutting machines or hand cutters and examine samples for defects.
Train workers to set up, operate, and use automatic bindery machines.
Repair, restore, or rebind old, rare, or damaged books using hand tools and perform highly skilled hand finishing operations.
Load digital images and processing materials into machines, ensuring they are prepared according to specifications.
Prepare finished books for shipping by wrapping, packing, and stacking boxes.
Design original or special bindings for limited editions or custom binding projects.
Compress, digitize, duplicate, and store audio and video data efficiently.
Confer with clients, printers, or designers to discuss job requirements or binding plans.
Monitor the quality of audio and video recordings and adjust equipment as necessary.
Perform necessary cleaning and maintenance of binding and printing equipment to ensure proper functioning.
Select proper types of plates according to press run lengths.
Examine finished plates to detect flaws and verify conformity with master plates, using light boxes and microscopes.
Operate presses to print proofs of plates, monitoring printing quality to ensure adequacy.
Operate and maintain laser plate-making equipment that converts electronic data to plates without the use of film.
Enter and arrange text size using computers to produce printed materials.
Create special effects, such as vignettes and combining images, and add elements to electronic publications.
Collaborate with graphic artists, editors, and writers to produce master copies according to specifications.
Select number of colors and determine color separations for printing jobs.
Prepare sample layouts for approval using computer software.
Load presses with paper and make necessary adjustments according to paper size.
Maintain, adjust, and clean printing equipment and perform minor repairs.
Start presses and pull proofs to check for ink coverage, density, alignment, and registration.
Inspect, test, or measure materials, products, or work for conformance to specifications.
Monitor inventory levels regularly, ordering additional supplies as necessary.
Monitor automated press operation systems and respond to fault or error messages.
Recommend necessary corrective actions based on inspection results.
Inspect job orders to determine quantities, stock specifications, colors, or special printing instructions.
Collect and inspect random samples during print runs to identify any necessary adjustments.
